task_completion.description: Verify that both the polygon and point layers are loaded into QGIS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers panel in the main QGIS window lists 'sample_polygon' and 'sample_points'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the layers are styled to be visually distinguishable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map canvas shows point markers clearly visible on top of the polygons, ideally with distinct colors or symbols.

task_completion.description: Verify that a print layout named 'Bay Area Report' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Print Layout window is visible, and its title bar or tab indicates the name 'Bay Area Report'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the print layout contains a map, a legend, a scale bar, and the specific title label.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layout canvas displays a map item showing the layers, a legend box, a scale bar, and a text label reading exactly 'Bay Area Monitoring Stations'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the layout is exported as a PNG image to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export as Image' dialog showing the path '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/bay_area_map.png', a success message indicating the export, or a file manager showing the exported file.

task_completion.description: Verify that the QGIS project is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project' dialog showing the path '/home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/bay_area_map.qgz', or the main QGIS window title bar displaying 'bay_area_map' as the current project.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the QGIS graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with QGIS menus, panels, and the Print Layout window, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts generating the files.

integrity.description: Verify that the actual QGIS application is being used.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard QGIS interface (toolbars, map canvas, panels) is visible.

integrity.description: Verify that the export process was genuinely performed within QGIS.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export dialog or layout manager in QGIS is used to save the PNG, confirming it wasn't just copied from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses synthetic GeoJSON data for the San Francisco Bay Area (WGS84, EPSG:4326) containing 2 polygon features and 3 point features. The print layout must be named 'Bay Area Report' and contain exactly four items: Map, Label ('Bay Area Monitoring Stations'), Legend, and ScaleBar. The project must be saved as 'bay_area_map.qgz' in '/home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/' and the layout exported as 'bay_area_map.png' in '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/'.
